content:
IVX HOLDINGS — FINAL 12-AI SENIOR DEVELOPER AUDIT, SECURITY FIX, DEPLOYMENT AND PRODUCTION VERIFICATION

OBJECTIVE

Complete the IVX 12-AI developer audit end to end, remove all unverified claims, fix the exposed-token security risk, verify every integration, assign permanent engineering roles, deploy the final system and return production evidence.

This is an execution task.

Do not return planning, narrative, estimates or “appears to work.”

No evidence = NOT VERIFIED.

==================================================
PHASE 0 — IMMEDIATE SECURITY ACTION
==================================================

A GitHub personal access token was exposed in the conversation screenshot.

Immediately:

1. Treat the exposed ghp_ token as compromised.
2. Revoke or disable that exposed token.
3. Do not print the token again.
4. Do not include secret values in logs, screenshots, reports or responses.
5. Verify whether the token was ever stored in:
   - Source code
   - Git history
   - Application logs
   - Chat history
   - Database records
   - Build logs
   - Deployment logs
6. If found in Git history, remove it securely and document the remediation.
7. Confirm the production runtime uses only an approved encrypted secret.
8. Return only:
   - Token status: REVOKED / NOT FOUND / ROTATION REQUIRED
   - Storage location checked
   - Secret-binding status
   - Authentication HTTP status
   - Redacted credential identifier

Do not request another credential unless the approved stored credential is proven missing, expired, revoked or returns 401/403.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — VERIFY THE CURRENT PRODUCTION CLAIMS
==================================================

Independently re-run every reported check.

Verify:

1. Owner login
2. Production /health
3. Production /version
4. GitHub authentication
5. GitHub repository binding
6. GitHub branch binding
7. GitHub HEAD commit
8. Render service binding
9. Render deployment status
10. Supabase connection
11. AI provider authentication
12. Task ledger availability
13. Android APK availability
14. Web production availability
15. Owner-only authorization

For each check return:

- Check name
- Exact command or request used
- Timestamp
- HTTP status or exit code
- Expected result
- Actual result
- PASS or FAIL
- Trace ID where available
- Evidence location

Do not reuse old results.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — RESOLVE THE CONTRADICTORY AUDIT STATUS
==================================================

The previous report states “8/9 PASS” while displaying nine PASS results.

Correct this inconsistency.

Recalculate all audit totals directly from the evidence.

Return:

- Total checks
- Passed checks
- Failed checks
- Blocked checks
- Not verified checks
- Correct final score

Do not report 9/9 unless all nine checks have current evidence.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — AUDIT ALL 12 EXISTING AI AGENTS
==================================================

Discover the real 12 IVX AI agents from configuration, code, database records, prompts and execution logs.

For every AI return:

- AI ID
- Current name
- Current system prompt location
- Assigned tools
- Repository permissions
- Database permissions
- Deployment permissions
- Test-runner permissions
- Modules owned
- Current task
- Last real execution
- Last files changed
- Last commit SHA
- Last test result
- Last deployment ID
- Last production verification
- Current blocker
- Whether it can execute or only generate narrative

Do not infer abilities from the agent name.

Use:

EVIDENCE UNAVAILABLE — NOT VERIFIED

for every unsupported claim.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — PRACTICAL SENIOR-DEVELOPER TEST
==================================================

Test each AI with one controlled task in its engineering specialty.

Every AI must demonstrate:

1. Repository inspection
2. Correct file identification
3. Root-cause analysis
4. Safe code modification
5. No placeholder implementation
6. Automated test creation or update
7. Test execution
8. Failure correction
9. Commit creation
10. Code review
11. Staging deployment where applicable
12. Production verification where approved
13. Rollback instructions
14. Evidence reporting

An AI automatically fails senior classification if it:

- Only returns narrative
- Does not modify real code
- Claims tests without output
- Claims deployment without a deployment ID
- Fabricates a commit SHA
- Uses mocked production success
- Exposes credentials
- Edits unrelated modules
- Cannot identify changed files
- Cannot recover from a failed test

==================================================
PHASE 5 — SENIORITY SCORING
==================================================

Score every AI across these categories:

- Codebase comprehension: 10 points
- Architecture judgment: 10 points
- Production code quality: 15 points
- Debugging and root-cause analysis: 10 points
- Database and migration safety: 10 points
- Security and authorization: 10 points
- Automated testing: 10 points
- Git and integration discipline: 10 points
- Deployment and rollback: 10 points
- Evidence accuracy and honesty: 5 points

Classification:

- 85–100: SENIOR
- 65–84: MID-LEVEL
- 40–64: JUNIOR
- 0–39: NOT A DEVELOPER AGENT

Do not force all 12 agents into SENIOR status.

Assign the score supported by evidence.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — FINAL IVX ENGINEERING ROLE MAP
==================================================

Assign one permanent primary role to each agent based on demonstrated capability:

AI 1 — Chief Software Architect
AI 2 — React Native Mobile Developer
AI 3 — Web Frontend Developer
AI 4 — Backend API Developer
AI 5 — Database and Supabase Developer
AI 6 — Authentication and Security Developer
AI 7 — Chat and Realtime Developer
AI 8 — Media and Reels Developer
AI 9 — DevOps and Cloud Developer
AI 10 — QA Automation Developer
AI 11 — Performance and Reliability Developer
AI 12 — Code Review, Integration and Evidence Developer

For every assignment return:

- AI ID
- Permanent role
- Seniority level
- Capability score
- Owned modules
- Owned directories
- Allowed tools
- Restricted actions
- Required reviewing AI
- Required QA AI
- Required deployment approval

Prevent overlapping ownership and simultaneous edits to the same files.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — IMPLEMENT THE SHARED TASK LEDGER
==================================================

Create one production-backed ledger for all 12 agents.

Each task must include:

- Task ID
- Title
- Module
- Assigned AI
- Reviewing AI
- QA AI
- Priority
- Status
- Start timestamp
- Last activity timestamp
- Files changed
- Database migrations
- API routes changed
- Test command
- Test output
- Commit SHA
- Pull request
- Deployment ID
- Environment
- Production URL
- Verification result
- Trace ID
- Blocker
- Remaining work

Allowed statuses:

- NOT STARTED
- ANALYZING
- IN PROGRESS
- CODE COMPLETE
- REVIEW REQUIRED
- TEST FAILED
- TEST PASSED
- DEPLOYMENT FAILED
- DEPLOYED
- PRODUCTION VERIFIED
- BLOCKED
- REJECTED

Only PRODUCTION VERIFIED counts as completed.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — BUILD THE OWNER COMMAND CENTER
==================================================

Create and deploy an Owner-only page:

IVX AI ENGINEERING COMMAND CENTER

Show all 12 AI agents with:

- Agent name
- Permanent role
- Seniority level
- Capability score
- Current task
- Status
- Real progress percentage
- Last activity
- Last file changed
- Last commit SHA
- Last test result
- Last deployment ID
- Current blocker
- Tasks completed today
- Failed tasks today
- Production-verified tasks
- Time since last activity

Add drill-down evidence for every task.

Add filters:

- Agent
- Role
- Seniority
- Module
- Status
- Date
- Failed
- Blocked
- Deployed
- Production verified

Owner authorization must be enforced server-side.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — AI PROVIDER AUTHENTICATION
==================================================

A previous production error reported:

providerError=Unauthenticated
AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
Origin: backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts

Verify and fix:

1. Active provider configuration
2. AI gateway configuration
3. Runtime environment binding
4. Correct variable name
5. Correct production environment
6. Provider authentication
7. Model availability
8. Request headers
9. Timeout handling
10. Retry handling
11. Structured error logging
12. Trace ID propagation

Do not print any secret.

Return:

- Root cause
- Exact provider HTTP status
- Environment verified
- Binding verified
- Fix applied
- Post-fix test
- Trace ID
- Production result

==================================================
PHASE 10 — GITHUB, RENDER AND SUPABASE VERIFICATION
==================================================

GITHUB

Verify:

- Repository owner
- Repository name
- Active branch
- HEAD SHA
- Runtime credential authentication
- Read permission
- Push permission
- Branch protection
- Latest IVX-created commit

RENDER

Verify:

- Service name
- Service ID
- Bound repository
- Bound branch
- Current deployment ID
- Current live commit
- Deployment status
- /health response
- /version response
- Rollback target

SUPABASE

Verify:

- Project binding
- Database connectivity
- Required tables
- Migrations
- Row-level security
- Owner authorization
- Realtime status
- Backup status

Production must satisfy:

GitHub HEAD SHA = Render deployed SHA = /version SHA

If these do not match, report DEPLOYMENT MISMATCH and correct it.

==================================================
PHASE 11 — ANDROID APK FINALIZATION
==================================================

Generate and verify the final Android build.

Required output:

- APK filename
- APK version
- Package ID
- Build ID
- Commit SHA
- Build timestamp
- File size
- SHA-256 checksum
- Minimum Android version
- Direct authenticated download location
- Installation test result
- Owner-login test result
- Feed test result
- Chat test result
- Media-upload test result

Do not claim the APK is verified until it has been installed on a real Android device or approved emulator.

If real-device testing cannot be executed, report:

BLOCKED — REAL ANDROID DEVICE TEST REQUIRED

Do not mark it PASS.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — REQUIRED FINAL REPORT
==================================================

Return exactly these sections:

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

- Total agents discovered
- Senior agents
- Mid-level agents
- Junior agents
- Non-developer agents
- Agents with repository execution
- Agents with testing capability
- Agents with deployment capability
- Agents with production proof
- Security incidents corrected
- Remaining blockers

2. CURRENT PRODUCTION STATUS

- Repository
- Branch
- GitHub HEAD SHA
- Render service
- Deployment ID
- Live SHA
- /health status
- /version status
- Supabase status
- AI provider status
- APK status

3. 12-AI AUDIT TABLE

| AI | Current Name | Score | Level | Permanent Role | Last Commit | Last Test | Last Deploy | Production Verified |

4. ACCESS MATRIX

| AI | GitHub Read | GitHub Write | Supabase | Render | Vercel | AWS | Expo | Test Runner | Production |

5. PRACTICAL TEST RESULTS

For each AI:

- Assigned test
- Files changed
- Tests executed
- Result
- Commit SHA
- Deployment ID
- Production evidence
- Final classification

6. ROLE OWNERSHIP MAP

List every agent, role, modules, directories and review requirements.

7. SECURITY REPORT

Include:

- Exposed token remediation
- Secret scan result
- Authentication result
- Authorization result
- Remaining security risks

8. DEPLOYMENT PROOF

Include:

- Commit SHA
- Deployment ID
- Build ID
- Health response
- Version response
- Production timestamp
- Rollback target

9. APK PROOF

Include:

- Filename
- Version
- Checksum
- Download location
- Installation result
- Device used
- Test result

10. REMAINING BLOCKERS

For every blocker include:

- Exact blocker
- Responsible AI
- Evidence
- Required action
- Whether owner action is truly required

==================================================
FINAL ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
==================================================

This task is complete only when:

1. The exposed GitHub token is remediated.
2. All previous production claims are reverified.
3. The incorrect audit score is corrected.
4. All 12 AI agents are discovered.
5. All 12 receive practical tests.
6. All 12 receive evidence-based scores.
7. All 12 receive permanent engineering roles.
8. The task ledger is production-backed.
9. The Owner Command Center is deployed.
10. AI provider authentication works.
11. GitHub, Render and Supabase bindings are verified.
12. GitHub HEAD equals Render live SHA.
13. Tests pass.
14. Production health passes.
15. APK is generated.
16. APK installation status is honestly reported.
17. Every verified claim includes real evidence.
18. No secret is exposed.
19. No placeholder data is used.
20. The final report is delivered.

Do not report “12/12 SENIOR” unless every agent earns a score of at least 85 with practical evidence.

Start execution now and return the first result only after the security remediation and current production verification are complete.
